{"key_event":"PF-led urban opposition mobilization","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened as PF electoral gains, constitutional-reform activism, labor unrest, and urban protest networks increasingly constrained the MMD, though the NGO Act and executive dominance limited organized autonomy. State power also rose modestly with debt relief, copper-boom revenues, anti-corruption prosecutions, and orderly succession after Mwanawasa’s death, but weak mining bargaining power, donor dependence, and regulatory failures kept capacity only moderate.","society_change":0.5,"society_power":7.2,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":6.5}
